mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% within a single calendar month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with expanding retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery, which compresses input cost margins while boosting top-line growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 60-day forward returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a sustained increase in real GDP growth signals that corporate earnings can outpace higher discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized during consecutive Fed fund rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in housing starts reflects improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precedes broader economic expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month HOUST increases over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of trading days following a FEDFUNDS reading above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in non-farm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad-based economic acceleration, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined momentum of PAYEMS and INDPRO fails to produce a positive predictive rank correlation for 60-day forward returns in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates alongside weakening industrial output, stagflationary pressures compress equity valuations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% MoM and INDPRO falls > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPI and falling INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of historical instances over a 15-year sample.
